rulings.Case Title: LILY CHAN VS. JUN XIANG LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recuse Pltf'S Attorney Due To Conflict Of Interest;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT JIN FAN HE Motion To Recuse Plaintiff's Attorney Due To Conflict Of Interest. Motion to recuse is denied. No existence that Eastridge is currently representing defendant He. No evidence that a substantial relationship exists between the prior case and the instant case. The prior case was landlord/tenent and the instant case is construction defect. Request for stay is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN JOHNSON VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. No triable issue of material fact regarding breach of contract and breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff's employment was at-will, and undisputed that plaintiff signed acknowledgment stating he understood his employment as at-will. (UMF#8,9,10,13) Assuming plaintiff created a prima facie case as to race, age, and disability discrimination claims, defendant established a legitimate business reason for terminating plaintiff. (UMF 29, 40) Plaintiff does not establish pretextual reason for the termination. Plaintiff's declaration objections sustained as to #4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 16, 21, 22, 25, 30, 32 and 33. Portions contradictory to plaintiff's deposition stricken. (See D'Amico vs. Bd. of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 21.) Specific and substaintial evidence of discrimination. Bussey and Holmes declarations lack foundation and not specific. No evidence of protected activity to support plaintiff's retaliation claim. (UMFS #76-78). No triable issue of fact to support harassment cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. ROSS ROBINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff People Of The State Of California'S Order To Show Cause Re Seizure
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2009, line 8, Plaintiff People of the State of California's Order to Show Cause regarding Seizure (Per ex parte Order of 7-16-09). Motion for order to show cause regarding contempt is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff fails to show defendant's willful disobediance of the order. (See CCP section 1211.5 and Conn. vs. Superior Court (1987) 196 Cal.App. 3d 774, 784. Plaintiff's request for Order of seizure is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff fails to properly petition for relief requested. Plaintiff must also comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1112(a)(3) requiring a memorandum in support of motions. =(302/CWW)
